mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for corporate earnings, mechanically boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in over 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp, or the signal fails to predict direction in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 50% of triggered cases, or individual series outperform the interaction term in predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization, which directly increases corporate profit margins and yields positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive returns during recovery phases, with out-of-sample directional accuracy dropping below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust labor demand, supporting consumer spending forecasts and generating positive S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day forward returns, with a cumulative rank correlation below 0.01 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth surpassing 2.5% annually forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in over 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.5% threshold, invalidating the inflation-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in real GDP and retail sales validates sustainable aggregate demand growth, reducing recession probabilities and driving positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve predictive accuracy over single-series models, or forward returns turn negative in more than half of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a manufacturing contraction, triggering immediate profit downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in over 55% of historical instances, or the signal loses statistical significance after 2015.
